featureName: Fix Bucket Migration — Unify all storage paths to 'documents' bucket
description: The unified 'documents' bucket migration (20260220150000) was partial. 6 critical code paths still reference the deleted/missing 'intervention-documents' bucket, causing 404 'Bucket not found' errors when viewing tenant-uploaded attachments. All upload paths, signed URL generators, and DB defaults must migrate to the 'documents' bucket with the new path format {team_id}/{entity_id}/{filename}.

userStories.title: Migrate file-service.ts to 'documents' bucket (fixes tenant attachment bug)
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, when I open an attachment uploaded by a tenant during intervention creation, the document loads correctly instead of showing 'Bucket not found'.
acceptanceCriteria: getPublicUrl, createSignedUrl, cleanup, and delete all use 'documents' bucket
Fallback for old records: getSignedUrl and deleteInterventionDocument read storage_bucket from document record when available
userStories.notes: Migrated all 6 bucket references in file-service.ts. Path format changed from interventions/{id}/{file} to {team_id}/{id}/{file}. getSignedUrl now accepts storageBucket param. deleteInterventionDocument reads storage_bucket from DB record. Also added audio MIME types to validateFile().

userStories.title: Migrate conversations/upload to 'documents' bucket
userStories.description: As a user, when I upload a chat attachment in a conversation thread, the file is stored in the unified 'documents' bucket with the correct path format.
acceptanceCriteria: Storage path follows new format: {team_id}/{intervention_id}/{filename} (team_id from thread)
Cleanup and signed URL generation use 'documents' bucket
userStories.notes: Migrated all 5 bucket references. Path format now includes team_id from thread.team_id. Upload, DB insert, cleanup, and signed URL all use 'documents'.

userStories.title: Fix conversation-service.ts signed URLs to use storage_bucket column
userStories.description: As a user viewing chat attachments, signed URLs are generated from the correct bucket (read from the document's storage_bucket column) instead of being hardcoded.
acceptanceCriteria: enrichMessagesWithSignedUrls uses attachment.storage_bucket || 'documents' instead of hardcoded 'intervention-documents'
Old attachments in 'intervention-documents' bucket still work via fallback
New attachments in 'documents' bucket also work
userStories.notes: Changed hardcoded 'intervention-documents' to attachment.storage_bucket || 'documents' for dynamic bucket resolution.

userStories.title: Fix intervention modifier page signed URLs
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire editing an intervention, document previews load correctly from the bucket stored in the document record.
acceptanceCriteria: Signed URL generation uses doc.storage_bucket || 'documents' instead of hardcoded 'intervention-documents'

userStories.title: Fix resend-inbound webhook email attachment uploads
userStories.description: As a system processing inbound emails, email attachments are stored in the 'email-attachments' bucket (matching the download route) instead of the deleted 'intervention-documents' bucket.
acceptanceCriteria: Email attachment upload uses .from('email-attachments') (matching download route at /api/emails/[id]/attachments/[attachmentId])
userStories.notes: Fixed pre-existing mismatch: webhook was uploading to 'intervention-documents' but download route reads from 'email-attachments'. Now aligned. Path updated with team_id prefix.

userStories.title: Create DB migration to change intervention_documents default bucket
userStories.description: Change the intervention_documents.storage_bucket column default from 'intervention-documents' to 'documents' so any new records automatically use the correct bucket.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration file changes DEFAULT to 'documents'
Types regenerated with npm run supabase:types (if default affects types)
userStories.notes: Created migration 20260226100000_change_intervention_documents_default_bucket.sql. Also updated column comment to document the legacy bucket.
